html:
Lesser men defied the dragonlords of the Valyrian Freehold at their peril. Art by Magali Villeneuve

Dragonlords were the dragon-riding most powerful nobles of the Valyrian Freehold.[1] Some members of House Targaryen, such as Aegon the Conqueror, have been referred to as "Dragonlords"[2][3] after the Doom of Valyria and the civilization's destruction.

Contents

  • 1 Customs
  • 2 History
  • 3 Recent Events
    • 3.1 A Dance with Dragons
  • 4 Known dragonlords
  • 5 Quotes
  • 6 References

Customs[ ]

Leadership of the Valyrian Freehold was contested by twoscore noble families who normally gathered in Valyria.[4][1] According to a semi-canon source, the forty dragon-riding families controlled hundreds of dragons and lived in the Freehold and the Lands of the Long Summer. Some dragonlords occasionally visited colonies of Valyria (notably Lys, which they founded and used as a pleasure retreat[5]), but they did not settle in them permanently.[6]

Strong in sorcery,[7] the dragonlords of old are said to have used binding spells[8] and horns[9] to control their dragons, and they used dragonflame to reshape stone into decorative shapes.[10] Some scholars have written that the dragonlords considered themselves more powerful than gods.[11]

The dragonlords had pale skin, silver-gold hair, and eyes of purple, lilac, and pale blue.[5] The dragonlords were strikingly (some say inhumanly)[12] beautiful.[13] In order to keep their bloodlines pure, the dragonlords customarily wed brother to sister.[4] Heraldic banners were not used by dragonlords.[4]

Some dragonlords kept monkeys, apes, panther cubs, and parrots from the Summer Isles as pets.[14]

History[ ]

While Dragonstone is believed to have been the westernmost outpost of Valyria,[1] some maesters believe that the base of the Hightower was created by dragonlords thousands of years ago.[10] The dragonlords are not believed to have traveled as far east as the Five Forts, though at least one dragonlord was Empress of Yi Ti through her marriage to God-Emperor Chai Duq, who kept her dragon at his court.[15]

Almost all dragonlords died during the Doom of Valyria. Some in Lys and Tyrosh survived the catastrophe but were killed in its aftermath. The dragonlord Aurion led an army from Qohor to the ruins of Valyria, but he was never seen again.[1] Although not dragonriders, many Volantenes consider themselves the successors of the dragonlords.[16] Volantis waged war against rival Free Cities during the Century of Blood.[1]

The Targaryens were one of the great dragonlord families, but far from the most powerful.[4] They were the last dragonlord house to survive the Doom, as Aenar the Exile had led his family to Dragonstone twelve years prior.[1] Riding three dragons, the Targaryens eventually conquered most of Westeros during Aegon's Conquest.[4]

Galendro's The Fires of the Freehold describes the feuds of the dragonlords,[17] and Wroth of the Dragonlords is the first play by Phario Forel.[18]

Recent Events[ ]

A Dance with Dragons[ ]

Ysilla refers to the dragonlords of Valyria as lords of fire.[19]

Known dragonlords[ ]

  • Aurion
  • Belaerys family
  • House Targaryen

Quotes[ ]

The dragonlords of the old Freehold were strong in sorcery, and lesser men defied them at their peril.[7]

—the kindly man to Arya Stark

Our histories speak of the dragonlords of dread Valyria and the devastation that they wrought upon the peoples of Old Ghis.[20]

—Galazza Galare to Barristan Selmy
